{"action":"created","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801","id":2533972039,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM6XCVhH","number":55801,"title":"crashed caused by calling dlopen in find_library","user":{"login":"wgmitchener","id":2119449,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIxMTk0NDk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2119449?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener","html_url":"https://github.com/wgmitchener","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":4,"created_at":"2024-09-18T14:53:33Z","updated_at":"2024-09-20T12:55:50Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"<!--
If you have a question please search or post to our Discourse site: https://discourse.julialang.org.
We use the GitHub issue tracker for bug reports and feature requests only.

If you're submitting a bug report, be sure to include as much relevant information as
possible, including:

1. The output of `versioninfo()`
2. How you installed Julia
3. A minimal working example (MWE), also known as a minimum reproducible example

If you're experiencing a problem with a particular package, open an issue on that
package's repository instead.

Thanks for contributing to the Julia project!
-->

The `find_library` function in `libdl.jl` makes two calls to `dlopen` while searching for a library. It has been discovered that this causes problems when looking for the ROCm library `libamdhip64.so` on Fedora 40 Linux. ROCm links with LLVM, and calling `dlopen` on it twice causes LLVM to crash and report some inconsistency in its settings:
```
: CommandLine Error: Option 'disassemble' registered more than once!
LLVM ERROR: inconsistency in registered CommandLine options
```
See [this thread](https://discourse.julialang.org/t/failed-while-initializing-amdgpu-jl-with-llvm-17-and-rocm-6-1-on-fedora-40/118341) for where the problem shows up.

The problem is not specific to Julia. It can be reproduced in C by calling `dlopen` on the library, then `dlcose`, then `dlopen` again. See [this post](https://discourse.julialang.org/t/failed-while-initializing-amdgpu-jl-with-llvm-17-and-rocm-6-1-on-fedora-40/118341/3).

My guess is that as called in `find_library`, `dlopen` follwed by `dlcose` leaves some state from the library in the process's working memory, and this state causes confusion when `dlopen` is called on the library again.

Can `find_library` be re-implemented so as not to actually call `dlopen` and `dlclose` on the file once it's been found?

In general, programs will call `find_library` followed by `dlopen`, so if it's possible for `find_library` to change the state of the process so that a call to `dlopen` afterward might see leftover state and crash, it makes sense to me that `find_library` needs to be rewritten to not call `dlopen` at all.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2363675276","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801#issuecomment-2363675276","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801","id":2363675276,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6M4tKM","user":{"login":"wgmitchener","id":2119449,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIxMTk0NDk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2119449?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener","html_url":"https://github.com/wgmitchener","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-20T12:55:49Z","updated_at":"2024-09-20T12:55:49Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"I think the underlying issue is that `dlopen` doesn't just open a file, it also runs functions in the .so file that are marked with a particular attribute. Those functions can apparently make persistent changes to the executable state that are not undone by `dlclose`. In reading the man page for `dlopen`, I don't see anything that requires libraries to be written so that they can be opened and closed multiple times without error. This is the first time I've encountered a library that generates errors in this way.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2363675276/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

The `find_library` function in `libdl.jl` makes two calls to `dlopen` while searching for a library. It has been discovered that this causes problems when looking for the ROCm library `libamdhip64.so` on Fedora 40 Linux. ROCm links with LLVM, and calling `dlopen` on it twice causes LLVM to crash and report some inconsistency in its settings:
```
: CommandLine Error: Option 'disassemble' registered more than once!
LLVM ERROR: inconsistency in registered CommandLine options
```
See [this thread](https://discourse.julialang.org/t/failed-while-initializing-amdgpu-jl-with-llvm-17-and-rocm-6-1-on-fedora-40/118341) for where the problem shows up.

The problem is not specific to Julia. It can be reproduced in C by calling `dlopen` on the library, then `dlcose`, then `dlopen` again. See [this post](https://discourse.julialang.org/t/failed-while-initializing-amdgpu-jl-with-llvm-17-and-rocm-6-1-on-fedora-40/118341/3).

My guess is that as called in `find_library`, `dlopen` follwed by `dlcose` leaves some state from the library in the process's working memory, and this state causes confusion when `dlopen` is called on the library again.

Can `find_library` be re-implemented so as not to actually call `dlopen` and `dlclose` on the file once it's been found?

In general, programs will call `find_library` followed by `dlopen`, so if it's possible for `find_library` to change the state of the process so that a call to `dlopen` afterward might see leftover state and crash, it makes sense to me that `find_library` needs to be rewritten to not call `dlopen` at all.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2363683840","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801#issuecomment-2363683840","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/55801","id":2363683840,"node_id":"IC_kwDOABkWpM6M4vQA","user":{"login":"wgmitchener","id":2119449,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIxMTk0NDk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2119449?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener","html_url":"https://github.com/wgmitchener","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-09-20T13:00:00Z","updated_at":"2024-09-20T13:00:00Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"This may also be related: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/47565
It's a similar bunch of errors that apparently come from some kind of global symbol clash caused by opening multiple versions of LLVM.

However, that wouldn't explain why the [simple C example program](https://discourse.julialang.org/t/failed-while-initializing-amdgpu-jl-with-llvm-17-and-rocm-6-1-on-fedora-40/118341/3) triggers the error. Only one version of LLVM is ever involved in that.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/comments/2363683840/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

brew install --cask mactex-no-gui
pip install nbconvert PyPDF2

I can find nbconvert and pyPDF2 in my anaconda (base) environment. However, I failed to convert a very simple .ipynb file with print('hello world') to pdf. The error message is as follows. ","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/nbconvert/issues/2102/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"timeline_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/nbconvert/issues/2102/timeline","performed_via_github_app":null,"state_reason":null},"comment":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/nbconvert/issues/comments/2158795081","html_url":"https://github.com/jupyter/nbconvert/issues/2102#issuecomment-2158795081","issue_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/nbconvert/issues/2102","id":2158795081,"node_id":"IC_kwDOAgGDcc6ArJlJ","user":{"login":"wgmitchener","id":2119449,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIxMTk0NDk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2119449?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener","html_url":"https://github.com/wgmitchener","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"created_at":"2024-06-10T16:15:47Z","updated_at":"2024-06-10T16:15:47Z","author_association":"NONE","body":"nbconvert includes boilerplate code that uses package `ucs`.
There is some problem with this package and TeX engines like XeTeX that handle Unicode.

A workaround is to convert the .ipynb file to LaTeX.
Edit the .tex file and comment out or delete the line with `\usepackage[...]{ucs}`.
Then compile the .tex file directly.

I'm puzzled because in the file `share/jupyter/nbconvert/templates/latex/base.tex.j2` in this repository, the line for `\usepackage[...]{ucs}` is inside of `\ifPDFTex ...` which should fix this problem.
It looks like commit 5fe7c81 specifically fixed this problem, and it's dated 2021.
However, in version 7.16.0 of `nbconvert`, which is what I got with `pip install --user nbconvert`, that file is rather different and the line for `\usepackage[...]{ucs}` is executed unconditionally.

I ran `pip install --user -U nbconvert` and it updated to version 7.16.4 of `nbconvert`, and that version includes the fix, and I can convert my notebook directly to PDF.","reactions":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/jupyter/nbconvert/issues/comments/2158795081/reactions","total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"hooray":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"performed_via_github_app":null}}

{"action":"opened","issue":{"url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49479","repository_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia","labels_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49479/labels{/name}","comments_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49479/comments","events_url":"https://api.github.com/repos/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49479/events","html_url":"https://github.com/JuliaLang/julia/issues/49479","id":1681358976,"node_id":"I_kwDOABkWpM5kN4CA","number":49479,"title":"Documentation for DelimitedFiles doesn't show that it's now a separate package","user":{"login":"wgmitchener","id":2119449,"node_id":"MDQ6VXNlcjIxMTk0NDk=","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/2119449?v=4","gravatar_id":"","url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener","html_url":"https://github.com/wgmitchener","followers_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/followers","following_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/following{/other_user}","gists_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/gists{/gist_id}","starred_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/starred{/owner}{/repo}","subscriptions_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/subscriptions","organizations_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/orgs","repos_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/repos","events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/events{/privacy}","received_events_url":"https://api.github.com/users/wgmitchener/received_events","type":"User","site_admin":false},"labels":[],"state":"open","locked":false,"assignee":null,"assignees":[],"milestone":null,"comments":0,"created_at":"2023-04-24T14:06:34Z","updated_at":"2023-04-24T14:06:34Z","closed_at":null,"author_association":"NONE","active_lock_reason":null,"body":"<!--
If you have a question please search or post to our Discourse site: https://discourse.julialang.org.
We use the GitHub issue tracker for bug reports and feature requests only.

If you're submitting a bug report, be sure to include as much relevant information as
possible, including:

1. The output of `versioninfo()`
2. How you installed Julia
3. A minimal working example (MWE), also known as a minimum reproducible example

If you're experiencing a problem with a particular package, open an issue on that
package's repository instead.

Thanks for contributing to the Julia project!
-->

In the online documentation, versions [1.9.0-rc2](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1.9.0-rc2/) and [1.10-dev](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1.10-dev/), the [DelimitedFiles](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1.9.0-rc2/stdlib/DelimitedFiles/) module is still listed under the standard library.
However, according to the [release notes](https://docs.julialang.org/en/v1.9.0-rc2/NEWS/), this module is now in a separate package and must be installed separately.
Some explanation should be added to the documentation under the standard library, or perhaps the whole section on DelimitedFiles should be removed.

FYI: I just upgraded a machine from Fedora 37 to 38, and that upgraded julia from 1.8 to 1.9. It took me a few minutes to figure out that this change to DelimitedFiles is why my program suddenly wasn't working.
